content: {"notes": [{"url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.13595", "title": "The history and risks of reinforcement learning and human feedback", "summary": "Computer Science > Computers and Society [Submitted on 20 Oct 2023 (v1), last revised 28 Nov 2023 (this version, v2)] Title:The History and Risks of Reinforcement Learning and Human Feedback View PDFAbstract:Reinforcement learning from human feedback (RLHF) has emerged as a powerful technique to make large language models (LLMs) easier to use and more effective. A core piece of the RLHF process is the training and utilization of a model of human preferences that acts as a reward function for optimization. This approach, which operates at the intersection of many stakeholders and academic disciplines, remains poorly understood.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Computer Science > Computers and Society [Submitted on 20 Oct 2023 (v1), last revised 28 Nov 2023 (this version, v2)] Title:The History and Risks of Reinforcement Learning and Human Feedback View PDFAbstract:Reinforcement learning from human feedback", "RLHF reward models are often cited as being central to achieving performance, yet very few descriptors of capabilities, evaluations, training methods, or open-source models exist.", "Submission history From: Nathan Lambert [view email][v1] Fri, 20 Oct 2023 15:45:16 UTC (1,201 KB) [v2] Tue, 28 Nov 2023 18:16:11 UTC (2,597 KB) References & Citations export BibTeX citation Loading..."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Computer Science > Computers and Society [Submitted on 20 Oct 2023 (v1), last revised 28 Nov 2023 (this version, v2)] Ti"}, {"url": "https://arxiv.org/abs/2506.08266", "title": "Reinforcement learning from human feedback with high-confidence safety constraints", "summary": "Computer Science > Machine Learning [Submitted on 9 Jun 2025] Title:Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with High-Confidence Safety Constraints View PDF HTML (experimental)Abstract:Existing approaches to language model alignment often treat safety as a tradeoff against helpfulness, which can lead to unacceptable responses in sensitive domains. To ensure reliable performance in such settings, we propose High-Confidence Safe Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (HC-RLHF), a method that provides high-confidence safety guarantees while maximizing helpfulness. Similar to previous methods, HC-RLHF explicitly decouples human preferences into helpfulness and harmlessness (safety), which are learned by training a reward model and a cost model, respectively.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Computer Science > Machine Learning [Submitted on 9 Jun 2025] Title:Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with High-Confidence Safety Constraints View PDF HTML (experimental)Abstract:Existing approaches to language model alignment often treat sa", "Submission history From: Yaswanth Chittepu [view email][v1] Mon, 9 Jun 2025 22:03:56 UTC (13,289 KB) Current browse context: cs.LG References & Citations export BibTeX citation Loading..."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Computer Science > Machine Learning [Submitted on 9 Jun 2025] Title:Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback with High"}, {"url": "https://openreview.net/forum?id=2ThpyBegZH", "title": "Reinforcement learning from human feedback with high-confidence safety guarantees", "summary": "Existing approaches to language model alignment often treat safety as a tradeoff against helpfulness which can lead to unacceptable actions in sensitive domains. To ensure reliable performance in such settings, we propose High-Confidence Safe Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (HC-RLHF), a method that provides high-confidence safety guarantees while maximizing helpfulness. Similar to previous methods, HC-RLHF explicitly decouples human preferences regarding helpfulness and harmlessness (safety) and trains separate reward and cost models, respectively.", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["For our empirical analysis, we apply HC-RLHF to align three different language models (Qwen2-1.5B, Qwen2.5-3B, and LLaMa-3.2-3B) with human preferences."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: For our empirical analysis, we apply HC-RLHF to align three different language models (Qwen2-1.5B, Qwen2.5-3B, and LLaMa"}, {"url": "https://ieeexplore.ieee.org/abstract/document/10474163/", "title": "The inadequacy of reinforcement learning from human feedback\u2014radicalizing large language models via semantic vulnerabilities", "summary": "Introduction With the rapid evolution of large language models (LLMs) such as OpenAI's GPT-3 and its successors, the field of generative artificial intelligence (AI) has witnessed a profound shift, increasingly narrowing the cognitive gap between humans and machines [1], [2], [3]. Often offered as commercially accessible services or LLM as a service (LLMaaS), a term we have coined to mirror the existing concept software as a service (SaaS), these advanced models have found applications across a multitude of domains, from creative endeavors to customer service, thereby significantly elevating productivity [4], [5]. However, the commercial availability of LLMs has also opened new avenues for complex adversarial attacks, putting both their reliability and advantageous applications at risk [6], [7], [8], [9], [10].", "perspectives": ["Scientific"], "key_facts": ["Often offered as commercially accessible services or LLM as a service (LLMaaS), a term we have coined to mirror the existing concept software as a service (SaaS), these advanced models have found applications across a multitude of domains, from creat", "The risks associated with these LLMs have gone beyond mere unauthorized access or hallucination generation to encroach upon social and ideological landscapes [11].", "While it is somewhat straightforward to implement acceptable use policies (AUPs) and syntactic keyword filters, the complexity arises in devising robust measures for semantically filtering or banning adversarial or offensive content [10], [14], [15]."], "knowledge_contribution": "Evidence: Often offered as commercially accessible services or LLM as a service (LLMaaS), a term we have coined to mirror the exis"}, {"url": "https://books.google.com/books?hl=en&lr=&id=uXohEQAAQBAJ&oi=fnd&pg=PA135&dq=What+evidence+exists+that+reinforcement+learning+from+human+feedback+(RLHF)+intr+safety+adverse+events+long-term&ots=dmtHudedDZ&sig=VtsCF-azuPaF4OSR-Iju-pZU_fc", "title": "7 Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback (RLHF)", "summary": "INSUFFICIENT_CONTENT", "perspectives": [], "key_facts": [], "knowledge_contribution": ""}]}
